data.narrative:
Skyrora, a Space & Aerospace company at SPAC stage, is currently valued at $250M following $66M in total capital raised. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 9 private peers in the same sector and valuation tier, including Skyryse ($1.1B Series C), Starcloud ($1.1B SPAC), Apex Space ($1.0B Series D), Stoke Space ($1.0B Series D), and Isar Aerospace ($1.0B Series C). Public anchors in Space & Aerospace (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) trade at a median 53.2x price-to-sales but cannot be applied without disclosed revenue.

The peer-median method yields the primary valuation signal: $1.0B implied value across the 9-company cohort. The private comparable set ranges from $274M to $1.1B, with the median at $1.0B. This method is applicable and directly relevant given stage and sector alignment. Public-comp anchoring is not applicable due to absence of target revenue data, typical for private companies.

The analysis supports a triangulated range of $850M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $1.0B. The current $250M valuation sits materially below this range, representing a 240–360% delta to the implied midpoint. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory relative to peer cohorts, technical execution risk in launch systems, and stage-specific dilution from SPAC capitalization.
